name: Amitabha Buddha Practice (Pure Land Nianfo / Nembutsu)
primary_purpose: Rebirth in Sukhāvatī (the Western Pure Land); peace of mind; transition support for the deceased

mantras.use: Primary Sanskrit mantra — Om Ami Deva Hrīḥ
Chinese Nianfo — Namo Amituofo (南無阿彌陀佛)

visualizations: Amitabha Buddha appears in the Western Pure Land of Sukhāvatī, seated on a lotus throne
His body is radiant red (#DC143C), the color of the setting sun, glowing with infinite light
His hands rest in his lap in meditation mudrā, holding a monk's begging bowl filled with the nectar of immortality
He is surrounded by his two attendant bodhisattvas: Avalokiteśvara (right, holding a lotus) and Mahāsthāmaprāpta (left, holding a vase of light)
From his heart, beams of red and golden light radiate in all directions, touching all beings who have devotion
At the moment of reciting his name, visualize a lotus flower opening in Sukhāvatī with your name on it
For the dying or deceased: visualize Amitabha appearing before them, surrounded by the sangha of the Pure Land, inviting them to be reborn there
Red light from Amitabha's heart enters the crown of the practitioner/dying person, dissolving attachment and drawing the consciousness toward Sukhāvatī
benefits: Peace of mind in this life — freedom from anxiety and fear
Removal of obstacles at the time of death
Guarantee of rebirth in Sukhāvatī (the Pure Land) with continued practice
Support for the dying and deceased — smoothing the transition
Purification of desire and attachment (Amitabha transmutes desire into discriminating wisdom)
Connection to the Buddha of Infinite Light and Infinite Life
Direct path to liberation for lay practitioners (simple practice, profound result)
frequency_purpose: Lowest solfeggio frequency — safety, security, foundation, and physical ease
pure_land_features: Ground made of seven precious materials (gold, silver, lapis, crystal, agate, ruby, cornelian)
Eight lakes of water with eight excellent qualities
Lotus flowers of various colors blooming everywhere
Trees made of jewels that rustle with dharma teachings
No realms of suffering — no hell beings, hungry ghosts, or animals
All conditions support rapid progress toward Buddhahood
best_times: Continuously throughout daily life (nianfo can be recited silently during any activity)
Especially powerful at the time of death — for 49 days after death
On Amitabha's feast days (e.g. 17th day of 11th lunar month, the day of his enlightenment)
During times of grief, fear, or anxiety
dedication: By reciting the name of Amitabha, may I and all beings be reborn in Sukhāvatī, the Pure Land of Bliss. May we swiftly attain Buddhahood for the benefit of all.
